ip_outcome_note: The card is confirmed as featuring Vegeta via multiple retail listings, most conclusively an eBay listing for a 'Full Power Energy Vegeta JUDGE FOIL' promotional variant (same card, same art, name variant explicitly names Vegeta). The vision describes blue-and-white Saiyan armor with a white-gold aura and upswept spiky hair in a power-up stance — all consistent with Vegeta's canonical DBZ visual design. However, a note: BT1-080 is listed as a green Extra card in some sources (consistent with the Broly archetype cluster positioning around BT1-075 to 081). If Vegeta appears in a green card it may be as a support/event card rather than a Vegeta-color card — the core IP identity (Vegeta depicted) stands regardless of card color. Verified: Vegeta (Dragon Ball Z).

trivia_bullets.text: BT1-080 is an Extra card from Galactic Battle (BT1, July 2017), positioned in the green color cluster near the Broly archetype cards. Its confirmed character is Vegeta — evidenced by the promotional Judge Foil variant explicitly named 'Full Power Energy Vegeta' [eBay: Full Power Energy Vegeta JUDGE FOIL DBS BT1-080].
sources: eBay listing: Full Power Energy Vegeta JUDGE FOIL Dragon Ball Super Card Game DBS BT1-080

trivia_bullets.text: The card's counter mechanic (negate the attack, then KO an opponent Battle card with energy cost 1 or less) reflects the 'Full Power' framing — a total defensive burst that also punishes low-cost board presence. The Counter Attack keyword was part of the foundational DBS TCG rule structure introduced in BT1 [WebSearch: pricecharting and retailer card text for BT1-080].
sources: WebSearch: pricecharting.com BT1-080 Full Power Energy; Troll and Toad BT1-080 listing

trivia_bullets.text: Vegeta's 'Full Power' state in Dragon Ball Z refers to his attempt to match Cell's Perfect Form by pushing his Super Saiyan transformation beyond its normal limits — a strategy that temporarily worked but proved less efficient than Son Gohan's eventual ascent. The card's art of a power-up stance with white-gold aura and blue-white Saiyan armor is consistent with that arc [Wikipedia: Cell Saga / Dragon Ball Z anime].
sources: WebSearch: Dragon Ball Z Cell Saga Vegeta Full Power Super Saiyan

trivia_bullets.text: A Judge Foil promotional version of this card was produced — Judge Foils in the DBS card game were distributed to official tournament judges as recognition items, making this particular variant a collector's piece beyond its gameplay utility [eBay: Full Power Energy Vegeta JUDGE FOIL DBS BT1-080].
sources: eBay listing: Full Power Energy Vegeta JUDGE FOIL Dragon Ball Super Card Game DBS BT1-080
